summary: Personalized vitamin and supplement recommender. Multi-step quiz feeds a rule-based engine that produces explained recommendations, an explainable health score, drug/supplement interaction warnings, recommendation history, cost-optimized alternatives, and a shareable read-only result page. Built with Next.js 16 + Supabase, integrating a custom 'nutrition-domain' Claude Code Skill and a 'supplement-evidence' MCP server.
constraints: Stack is fixed: Next.js 16 (App Router, TS), Tailwind v4, Supabase (Postgres + magic-link auth), Vercel.

This is Next.js 16 / React 19 — newer than Claude's training cutoff. Before writing or editing any Next.js code, read the relevant guide in node_modules/next/dist/docs/ and respect the warnings in AGENTS.md.
Use the nutrition-domain Skill (.claude/skills/nutrition-domain/) whenever editing files under lib/engine/.
Use the supplement-evidence MCP server (.claude/mcp/supplement-evidence/) for citation lookups; the server runs at dev/build time only — its JSON data is bundled for runtime.
Every user-facing recommendation surface must show a medical disclaimer and respect age/pregnancy gating.
Per-user data is isolated via Supabase RLS — never bypass RLS in application code.
Use npm (not pnpm/yarn) — verification commands assume npm scripts.
Goals execute one at a time per the spec-driven workflow; flip status pending -> passed only after the goal's verification command passes.
assumptions: User has (or will create) a Supabase project and a Vercel account; they will paste env vars when goal 2 / 3 ask for them.
Curated supplement dataset of ~30 entries is sufficient for the demo (no external API integrations).
Demo audience is the AUM AI Agentic class in Week 16 of Spring 2026.
goal_selection_rule: Work the first pending goal whose dependencies are all passed.

goals.goal: Next.js 16 + TS + Tailwind scaffold with placeholder VitaPath landing page; spec.json, .claude/ skill+MCP scaffolding, and updated README all committed; live on Vercel.

checklist: git log shows initial 'chore: vitapath scaffold + spec.json' commit
https://vitamin-chi.vercel.app/ returns 200 and renders the VitaPath landing copy
spec.json present at repo root and parses as valid JSON

goals.goal: Supabase project linked to repo via env vars; supabase/migrations/0001_init.sql applied (profiles, quizzes, recommendations, share_tokens with RLS + policies; get_shared_result security-definer RPC for shareable results).

checklist: All 4 tables exist in public schema with RLS enabled
Per-table policy counts: profiles=3, quizzes=2, recommendations=2, share_tokens=2
get_shared_result(text) RPC exists and is grantable to anon

goals.goal: Magic-link auth flow: sign-in form, callback handler (PKCE code + token_hash verifyOtp), sign-out route; /history protected by proxy.ts and redirects unauthenticated users to /login.

tests_to_write: e2e: anonymous /history redirects to /login
e2e: login page renders the magic-link form
e2e: admin-minted magic link signs user in, /history shows email, sign-out re-protects /history
checklist: Sign-out clears the Supabase session cookie (verified by post-signout redirect back to /login)

goals.goal: nutrition-domain Skill scaffolded with SKILL.md and reference JSON files (nutrient_rdas.json, interactions.json, contraindications.json) that validate against a Zod schema in lib/engine/schemas.ts.

tests_to_write: All reference JSON files parse against their Zod schemas
Skill loads without errors when listed by Claude Code

goals.goal: supplement-evidence MCP server runs over stdio and exposes get_supplement, list_studies, search_evidence tools backed by data/supplements.json; registered in .mcp.json at repo root.

Multi-step quiz UI at /quiz/[step]: 5 sections (basics, diet, sleep, stress, lifestyle), URL-routed steps, Zod-validated forms, progress bar, server action persists answers to a draft quiz row on submit.

tests_to_write: Playwright walks all 5 steps end-to-end
Refresh on step 3 keeps answers from steps 1-2
Final submit creates a quizzes row visible in Supabase

goals.goal: Pure recommendation engine in lib/engine/rules.ts: takes typed quiz answers, returns Recommendation[] with rule_id, supplement_slug, dose, and rationale; uses nutrition-domain JSON as the source of truth.

tests_to_write: >=10 unit tests covering: low-sun-exposure -> vitamin D; vegan -> B12; high-stress -> magnesium; low-sleep -> magnesium glycinate; female 19-50 -> iron consideration; pregnancy -> folate; etc.
Engine is pure (same input -> same output, no I/O)

goals.goal: Health score function in lib/engine/score.ts returns { score: 0-100, contributions: [{ rule_id, delta, label }] } where contributions sum to score - baseline.

tests_to_write: Deterministic for fixed input
sum(contributions.delta) === score - baseline for 5 sampled inputs
Contributions are stable-sorted by absolute delta descending

goals.goal: Interaction checker in lib/engine/interactions.ts: given recommendations + user-disclosed medications/conditions, returns Warning[] with severity and explanation; pregnancy contraindications remove offending recs and add a warning.

tests_to_write: St John's Wort + SSRI -> high-severity warning
Vitamin K + warfarin -> high-severity warning
Iron + calcium taken together -> moderate timing warning
Pregnancy + retinol/vitamin A high dose -> rec removed + warning added
Pregnancy + folate -> rec retained, no warning
No interactions -> empty Warning[]

goals.goal: Results page at /results: rec cards, animated score gauge, contribution breakdown, interaction warnings, evidence citations sourced from supplement-evidence MCP data bundled at build time.

tests_to_write: Quiz -> results renders all sections
Each rec card shows >=1 evidence citation with study title and year
Score gauge value matches /api/score response

goals.goal: /history page lists past quizzes (date, score, # recs) and renders a Recharts line chart of score over time.

tests_to_write: After 2 quizzes, history shows 2 rows and 2 plotted points
Clicking a row deep-links to that quiz's frozen result view

goals.goal: Cost-optimized alternative for each recommendation: alternative selector picks the cheapest supplements.json brand meeting dose; UI toggle on each rec card swaps recommended brand <-> alternative.

tests_to_write: Selector returns cheapest brand whose dose >= recommended
Returns null when no alternative is cheaper than the primary
UI: clicking 'see alternative' updates price and brand text without page reload

goals.goal: Shareable read-only result page at /r/[token]: server action mints a token (unique, expires_at = now + 30 days) and the page renders without auth via a security-definer RPC.

tests_to_write: Authed user generates a share link from /results
Incognito visit to /r/[token] renders read-only results
Visit to /r/[expired-token] shows an 'expired' state and no data

goals.goal: Pre-quiz gating: hard-coded medical disclaimer modal must be acknowledged before /quiz/1 is accessible; under-18 selection blocks quiz with a friendly 'consult a pediatrician' page; pregnancy-status answer is captured up-front and threaded into the engine.

tests_to_write: Direct visit to /quiz/1 without acknowledging disclaimer redirects to /disclaimer
Selecting age < 18 navigates to a blocking explainer, not the quiz
Selecting pregnant: yes -> the engine receives pregnancy=true (asserted via a /api/debug/last-input route guarded behind NODE_ENV=development)

goals.goal: Final polish: README rewritten with setup, env vars, MCP/Skill docs; architecture diagram (PNG or mermaid in README); production Vercel deploy passing; Lighthouse landing >=90; demo checklist runs clean.

verification.command: npm test && npm run test:e2e && npm run build
checklist: npm run build completes with zero type errors
Live URL passes manual demo checklist (signup, quiz, pregnancy re-quiz, share link, history)
README has: install, env, run, deploy, MCP setup, Skill description, architecture diagram

definition_of_done: All 15 goals are passed (status = 'passed' in spec.json)
Production app reachable on Vercel and demo checklist green
